message:
perf(indexer): RegexSet pre-check + Cow return in redact_text

The harmless-input path for ingest-time secret redaction was:
  fn redact_text(input: &str) -> String {
      let mut output = input.to_string();   // always allocates
      for pat in SECRET_PATTERNS.iter() {   // 12 regex scans
          output = pat.regex.replace_all(&output, REDACTED).into_owned();
      }
      output
  }

The overwhelmingly common case — ordinary tool output with no
credentials — still paid one String allocation and twelve regex
scans per message.

Two changes collapse that to one scan + zero allocations:

1. A RegexSet built from the same 12 patterns handles the pre-check
   in a single pass. If RegexSet::matches() returns no hits we return
   Cow::Borrowed(input) immediately.

2. When there are hits, replace_all is only called for the indices
   RegexSet reported, promoting Cow to Owned only if an individual
   replace_all actually changed anything.

Signature change: redact_text now returns Cow<'_, str> instead of
String. Callers in src/indexer/mod.rs's persist::* paths append
.into_owned() at the four sites that need an owned copy (title,
metadata_json round-trip, message content, snippet text). redact_json
keeps its owned-String path via .into_owned() at two sites.

A new benches/index_perf.rs::bench_redact_text criterion group
locks in the hot-path regression guard with two inputs: a harmless
message (should be borrowed pass-through) and a message containing
an api_key + GitHub PAT (exercises the replace path).

A new mod-local test asserts that the no-secret path returns
Cow::Borrowed, so a future regression that silently reintroduces
allocation breaks CI instead of slipping through unnoticed.
